examples.title: Probability Calculator for Two Events
examples.description: Calculate union, intersection, and conditional probabilities given basic probabilities
examples.explanation: Demonstrates basic probability operations using Python. Calculates union using addition rule and conditional probability. Also checks if events are independent.

examples.title: Bayes' Theorem Implementation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate posterior probability using Bayes' theorem
examples.explanation: Implements Bayes' theorem to update probability based on new evidence. Commonly used in spam filtering, medical diagnosis, and machine learning classifiers.

examples.title: Simulation - Monte Carlo Probability Estimate
examples.description: Estimate probability through simulation (relative frequency approach)
examples.code:
import random

def simulate_dice_sum(target_sum=7, trials=100000):
    """Estimate P(sum=7) by simulation"""
    count = 0
    for _ in range(trials):
        die1 = random.randint(1, 6)
        die2 = random.randint(1, 6)
        if die1 + die2 == target_sum:
            count += 1
    return count / trials

# Run simulation
estimated_prob = simulate_dice_sum(7, 100000)
theoretical_prob = 6/36  # 6 favorable out of 36 outcomes

print(f"Estimated P(sum=7): {estimated_prob:.4f}")
print(f"Theoretical P(sum=7): {theoretical_prob:.4f}")
print(f"Difference: {abs(estimated_prob - theoretical_prob):.4f}")

# Output typically shows estimated ≈ 0.1667 (theoretical = 1/6)
examples.explanation: Demonstrates relative frequency approach - running many trials to estimate probability. As trials → ∞, estimate approaches true probability (Law of Large Numbers).

examples.title: Check Event Properties
examples.description: Java program to determine if events are mutually exclusive or independent
examples.explanation: Java program to verify properties of events. Shows how to mathematically determine if two events are mutually exclusive (intersection = 0) or independent (P(A∩B) = P(A)×P(B)).
